title: REQUEST FOR RECORDS RICKEY LEE ZARSKY

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Government Records Access and Management Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting any records in the possesion of Ogden Police Department from 01/01/2025 through present date 04/17/2026  for Rickey Lee Zarsky DOB 11/22/1953

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Annemarie Grant

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Request for certain purchase records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Purchase records, invoices, procurement documents and other documents sufficient to show any and all disbursement of public funds for the acquisition by your agency since July 1, 2023 of any of the following:
• Firearms of .50 caliber or greater
• Patrol rifles (as commonly denominated for law enforcement use) or assault rifles (as defined in Sections 30510 and 30515 of the California Penal Code)
• Projectile launch platforms, such as 40mm projectile launchers, "bean bag" or specialty impact munition ("SIM") weapons, and "riot guns" used to disperse chemical agents
• Explosives and pyrotechnics, such as "flash bang" grenades and explosive breaching tools, 37mm and 40mm 'less lethal' munitions, and chemical weapons such as "teargas", "pepper balls", "OC spray" and related items.

2. Records of deployment by your agency since July 1, 2023 of projectile launch platforms and munitions, such as 40mm projectile launchers, "bean bag" or specialty impact munition ("SIM") weapons, and "riot guns" used to disperse chemical agents; explosives and pyrotechnics, such as "flashbang" grenades and explosive breaching tools; and chemical weapons such as "teargas" and "pepper balls".

3. Records that include a glossary and/or explanation for "Purchasing Group" initials or abbreviations, as used in the CDCR purchases (see column D in "CDCRArmory_Spreadsheet" file of CDCR response to Public Records Act request # M003971-122821 in January 2022).

4. Records that indicate the number and category (e.g. APP, CCCMS, EOP, RHE, ICF-high, ICF-low, etc) of mental health inmates, for each CDCR facility, in 2025. 

Please reference CDCR's responses to Public Records Act requests # M003971-122821 in January 2022 and # C011884-071723 in August 2023. For #1 through #3 above, we request records of the same type and in a similar format as for those requests. 

If you have questions about this request, please don't hesitate to contact me directly via phone, 510-282-8983 or email, jlindsay-poland@afsc.org.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Lindsay-Poland
